title: Lightly Fried Turkish Lamb Kebabs
description: A tangy and festive Turkish dish featuring lamb, treacle, and a light agemono-style deep fry, making it a low-fat friendly option.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together treacle, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Place the skewers on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 15 minutes.
While the lamb is baking, heat about 1/2 inch of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Remove the lamb from the oven and carefully place the skewers in the hot oil, frying for 2-3 minutes on each side.
Remove the skewers from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with chopped parsley.
